mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in <55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single quarter signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth across services and construction, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index values reflect stronger manufacturing and utility output, which correlates with improved corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO levels or changes over a 24-month walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation runs above 3.0 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0 percent, real rates turn deeply negative, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative 20d returns occur in <40% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% and FEDFUNDS stays below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher during periods of positive PAYEMS growth compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5 percent indicates an immediate economic slowdown that reduces near-term corporate cash flow expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts by more than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment reflects robust supply-side momentum without labor market overheating, creating favorable conditions for positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20d returns with an information coefficient above 0.05 in walk-forward validation.
